title: Deteksi Website Phishing Menggunakan Teknik Machine Learning
abstract: Phishing merupakan teknik penipuan yang memanfaatkan penyamaran sebagai entitas tepercaya untuk mencuri data sensitif. Metode deteksi berbasis machine learning, seperti XGBoost, Random Forest, dan Decision Tree, efektif dalam mengenali pola website phishing. Hasil penelitian menunjukkan bahwa XGBoost dengan hyperparameter tuning memberikan akurasi tertinggi, yaitu 96%. Model ini kemudian diimplementasikan dalam web service menggunakan Flask atau FastAPI, sehingga pengguna dapat memeriksa URL secara real-time. Sistem juga dilengkapi mekanisme ekstraksi fitur, termasuk analisis URL, domain age, dan konten halaman, serta pelabelan otomatis untuk memudahkan pelatihan ulang model. Selain itu, integrasi dengan bot Telegram memperluas aksesibilitas, karena pengguna dapat melakukan deteksi phishing kapan saja melalui pesan instan tanpa batasan lokasi atau perangkat
title -English: Detecting Phishing Websites Using Machine Learning Techniques
abstract - English: Phishing is a fraudulent technique that involves impersonating a trusted entity to steal sensitive data. Machine learning-based detection methods, such as XGBoost, Random Forest, and Decision Tree, are effective at identifying patterns in phishing websites. Research results show that XGBoost with hyperparameter tuning achieves the highest accuracy, at 96%. This model is then implemented as a web service using Flask or FastAPI, allowing users to check URLs in real time. The system also includes feature extraction mechanisms—such as URL analysis, domain age, and page content—as well as automatic labeling to facilitate model retraining. Additionally, integration with a Telegram bot expands accessibility, as users can perform phishing detection at any time via instant messaging without restrictions on location or device.

title: Energías Renovables: sostenibilidad para todos
abstract: Poco más de 20 años después de la declaraciónde Río de Janeiro, realizada por las Naciones Unidas,conocida como Agenda 21, varios de los preceptosy objetivos planteados siguen siendo una quimera.El hecho de que miles de millones de personas hoyen día carezcan de acceso a la electricidad y siganutilizando el fuego para preparar los alimentos esalarmante. Peor aún, esta situación cambiará muypoco en los próximos años. Las fuentes de energíano renovables no representan una solución adecuadapara los países en desarrollo, donde la mayoría de lagente vive sin acceso a la energía. El uso de energíarenovable en los países en desarrollo tiene un granpotencial para cambiar ciertas variables, como lapobreza y el hambre, el acceso a la educación, ladesigualdad de género, la mortalidad infantil, la saludmaterna y la morbilidad de la población
title -English: Renewable Energy: Sustainability for All
abstract - English: A little over 20 years after the United Nations’ Rio de Janeiro Declaration—known as Agenda 21—several of the principles and objectives set forth remain a pipe dream. The fact that billions of people today lack access to electricity and continue to use fire to cook their food is alarming. Worse still, this situation will change very little in the coming years. Non-renewable energy sources do not represent an adequate solution for developing countries, where most people live without access to energy. The use of renewable energy in developing countries has great potential to address issues such as poverty and hunger, access to education, gender inequality, infant mortality, maternal health, and overall population morbidity.

title: Nearly-zero-energy-buildings
abstract: Det nya EU-direktivet EPBD2, Energy Performance of Buildings Directive (recast), som antogs 2010, specificerar att alla nya byggnader ska vara så kallade nära-nollenergibyggnader år 2021. En nära-nollenergibyggnad definieras som en byggnad med mycket hög energiprestanda, som till mycket hög grad förses med förnybar energi. Hur detta ska tolkas och var kravnivån ska sättas för energianvändningen är upp till varje land att besluta. I Sverige pågår just nu en debatt om detta och Energimyndigheten har fått i uppdrag att  utforma en svensk strategi för att möta kravet om nära-nollenergibyggnader. Nära sammanlänkat med detta är också miljömålen om minskad klimatpåverkan till 2020 och 2050. För att sätta en rimlig kravnivå för energianvändning måste både ekonomiska-, tekniska och miljöaspekter tas hänsyn till. I denna rapport undersöks ett planerat bostadsområde i Västra Sörhaga, Alingsås, för att utvärdera hur det skulle kunna bebyggas för att nå upp till standarden för näranollenergibyggnader. En större undersökning görs också för att studera en rimlig kravnivå för nära-nollenergibyggnader i hela landet. Energiberäkningsprogrammet VIP-energy används för detta ändamål, fyra byggnader i olika storlekar simuleras. Var man sätter systemgränsen har stor inverkan på resultatet vid bedömningen av en byggnads energianvändning. EPBD2 fastställer att byggnadens energiprestanda ska prioriteras före tillförseln av förnybara energikällor. För att uppnå en byggnad med god energiprestanda krävs först en optimerad byggnadsprestanda (vilken innefattar de mest långlivade delarna av byggnaden, t.ex. klimatskalet), sedan ett effektivt energisystem och en effektiv energitillförsel. När detta är uppnått kan man sedan fokusera på att den tillförda energin i så hög grad som möjligt ska vara förnybar. Det är således viktigt att först optimera byggnadens prestanda, för att sedan väga in energitillförselsystem och energikälla varmed byggnaden också förses med så låg primärenergi och så mycket förnybar och miljövänlig energi som möjligt. Energimyndighetens strategi föreslår ett energikrav som mål för nära-nollenergibyggnader på halva dagens energikrav i Boverkets byggregler. Resultaten visar att det skulle innebära ganska höga krav för små byggnader längst upp i norr. Ett exempel på åtgärder som skulle innebära att denna nivå uppnås även för en villa i Kiruna är eliminering av köldbryggor, värmeväxling av ventilationsluften med 90 % verkningsgrad, fönster med u-värde på 0.7 W/m2K, dörrar med 0.6 W/m2K, täthet på 0.2 l/s,m2 och en fönsterandel på 10 %. Detta ligger i överkant med vad som är möjligt att uppnå med dagens teknik. Värmeväxlare med 90 % verkningsgrad finns idag, men eftersom värmeväxlare behöver avfrostas i kallt klimat, vilket sänker den verkliga verkningsgraden, kan det vara svårt att uppnå denna verkningsgrad praktiken i Kiruna. Med framtida teknikutveckling och större erfarenheter av lågenergibyggnationer är dock energimyndighetens förslag en rimlig standard för näranollenergibyggnader i hela landet. Det skulle dock vara fördelaktigt med en uppdelning av landet i fler klimatzoner, eller åtminstone en jämnare uppdelning, eftersom variationen i energianvändning för likadana byggnader idag är betydligt större i den nordligaste klimatzonen än i den sydligaste. I de södra delarna av Sverige skulle det vara möjligt att sätta passivhusstandarden som kravnivå för nära-nollenergibyggnader, men det rekommenderas inte i de norra delarna eftersom speciellt små byggnader i dagsläget är mycket svåra att bygga i passivhusstandard i kallt klimat. Att uppnå passivhusstandard för villor i den nordligaste delen av landet skulle kunna kräva en inomhustemperatur som inte överstiger 20°C och en täthet på 0. l/s,m2. Detta är inte rimligt, främst för att det enligt brukardata är vanligt med högre inomhustemperaturer i Sverige och för att en så hög täthet inte kan garanteras idag. Området i Västra Sörhaga, Alingsås, är däremot realistiskt att bygga i passivhusstandard eftersom det ligger i klimatzon III och det skulle då med största säkerhet också uppfylla standarden för näranollenergibyggnader. Om området byggdes i passivhusstandard skulle det resultera i en total specifik energianvändning i storleksordningen 50 kWh/m2,år, och en total energianvändning som motsvarar 45 % av energianvändningen för ett likadant område som uppfyller dagens energikrav i Boverkets byggregler. Kring 180 000 kWh/år skulle kunna tas från fjärrvärme. Boverkets förslag till revideringen av energikraven 2011 bedöms inte vara tillräckligt ambitiösa och gör det mycket svårt att uppnå miljömålen till 2020. Ytterligare studier behöver göras på den ekonomiska lönsamheten för lågenergibyggander, och för att kunna värdera den tillförda energin på ett enhetligt sätt behöver primärenergifaktorer tas fram. Dessa bör inkludera både effektivitet i framställningen, energiform och energislag. Energislagen bör i sin tur bedömas efter grad av förnybarhet, tillgång och lagringsmöjlighet. Lågenergibebyggelse kräver en stor omställning av den befintliga byggmarknaden och energistrukturen. För att hinna med detta till 2021 måste vi börja nu!The new EU directive EPBD2, Energy Performance of Buildings Directive (recast), adopted 2010, specifies that all new buildings must be so-called nearly-zero-energy-buildings by year 2021. A nearly-zero-energy-building is a building with very high energy performance, which is provided with renewable energy to a large extent. How this should be interpreted and where the requirement is to be placed on energy usage is up to each country to decide. There is currently a debate about this going on in Sweden, and the Swedish Energy Agency has been asked to design a strategy to meet the goals for nearly-zero-energy-buildings. The environmental goal for reducing the carbon footprint by 2020 and 2050 is also closely linked to this question. In order to set a reasonable level of requirement for energy use, both economic-, technical- and environmental aspects must be taken into account. This report studies a new building area in Västra Sörhaga, Alingsås, to find out how to build it in order to achieve the standard for nearly-zero-energy-buildings. A wider study is also made to find out where it is reasonable to set the level of requirements for energy use for nearly-zero-energybuildings throughout the country. The energy calculation software VIP-energy is used for simulating four buildings, off different sizes, and studying their energy consumption. Where the system boundary is placed has a big impact on the outcome of the measurement of a building's energy use. EPBD2 determines that the building's energy performance should be a priority, before the supply of renewable energy sources. In order to attain a building with a high energy performance, an optimized construction performance is first required, with an airtight and highly insulated building envelope and a ventilation system with heat recovery, and then an efficient energy system and an efficient energy supply should be ensured. When this is reached, the focus can then be put on making sure that the energy supplied is renewable to as large extent as possible. It is therefore important to optimize the building performance first and then take the energy supply system and energy sources into account, whereby the building also has a low primary energy use and is supplied with as much renewable and environmentally friendly energy as possible. The Swedish Energy Agency’s proposes an energy requirement of half the current energy requirements as a goal for nearly-zero-energy-buildings. One example of energy reducing measures that would result in achieving this standard for a cottage in Kiruna is: elimination of thermal bridges, heat exchange of the ventilation air with 90 % efficiency, windows with a heat transfer coefficient of 0.7 W/m2K and doors with 0.6 W/m2K, airtightness of 0.2 l/s,m2 and a window proportion of 10 %. This is a stretch of what is possible to achieve with today’s technology. Heat exchangers with an efficiency of 90 % exist today, but since they require defrosting in cold climate, which decreases the real efficiency, it could be difficult to achieve in Kiruna. However, with future technological developments and experiences of building low energy buildings, this would be a reasonable standard for nearly-zero-energy-buildings throughout the country. It would also be advantageous to divide the country into more climate zones, or at least divide them more equally. The variation in energy use for a building is today much larger in the most northern climate zone than in the southernmost. thermal bridges, heat exchange of the ventilation air with 90 % efficiency, windows with a heat transfer coefficient of 0.7 W/m2K and doors with 0.6 W/m2K, airtightness of 0.2 l/s,m2 and a window proportion of 10 %. This is a stretch of what is possible to achieve with today’s technology. Heat exchangers with an efficiency of 90 % exist today, but since they require defrosting in cold climate, which decreases the real efficiency, it could be difficult to achieve in Kiruna. However, with future technological developments and experiences of building low energy buildings, this would be a reasonable standard for nearly-zero-energy-buildings throughout the country. It would also be advantageous to divide the country into more climate zones, or at least divide them more equally. The variation in energy use for a building is today much larger in the most northern climate zone than in the southernmost. It would be possible to use the passive house standard requirements for nearly-zero-energybuildings in the southern parts of Sweden, but it is not recommended in the north. Especially small buildings are very difficult to build in the passive house standard in cold climates today. To reach passive house standard for a small building in the northern most part of Sweden could require an indoor temperature no higher than 20°C and an airtightness of 0.1 l/s,m2. This is not reasonable, mostly because it is common to have a higher indoor temperature in Sweden and because this very high air tightness cannot be guaranteed today. The area in Västra Sörhaga, Alingsås, is realistic to build with requirements of passive house standard because it is in climate zone III, and that would almost certainly also satisfy the standard for nearly-zero-energy-buildings. If the area was built in the passive house standard it would result in a total specific energy consumption of around 50 kWh/m2 per year, which is 45 % of the energy use for the same area built to meet today’s energy requirements. The national board of housing’s proposed energy requirements for the revision of the construction rules for 2011 are deemed not to be ambitious enough and makes it very difficult to achieve environmental objectives by 2020. Further studies need to be made on the economic viability of buildings with low energy use, and in order to assess the amount of energy in a uniform manner, primary energy factors need to be developed. These should include both efficiency in manufacturing, energy form and energy sources. Energy sources, in turn, should be judged by the degree of renewability, access and storage capacity. Construction of low-energy buildings requires a large change in the existing construction market and energy structure. We have to start now to achieve this by 2021
title -English: Nearly-zero-energy-buildings
abstract - English: The new EU directive EPBD2, the Energy Performance of Buildings Directive (recast), which was adopted in 2010, specifies that all new buildings must be so-called nearly zero-energy buildings by 2021. A nearly zero-energy building is defined as a building with very high energy performance, powered to a very high degree by renewable energy. How this is to be interpreted and where the threshold for energy consumption should be set is up to each country to decide. In Sweden, a debate on this issue is currently underway, and the Swedish Energy Agency has been tasked with developing a Swedish strategy to meet the requirement for nearly zero-energy buildings. Closely linked to this are also the environmental goals to reduce climate impact by 2020 and 2050. To set a reasonable standard for energy use, economic, technical, and environmental aspects must all be taken into account. This report examines a planned residential area in Västra Sörhaga, Alingsås, to evaluate how it could be developed to meet the standard for nearly zero-energy buildings. A broader study is also conducted to determine a reasonable standard for nearly zero-energy buildings nationwide. The VIP-energy energy calculation program is used for this purpose, and four buildings of various sizes are simulated. Where the system boundary is set has a significant impact on the results when assessing a building’s energy use. EPBD2 stipulates that a building’s energy performance must be prioritized over the supply of renewable energy sources.  To achieve a building with good energy performance, it is first necessary to optimize the building’s performance (which includes the most durable parts of the building, such as the building envelope), followed by an efficient energy system and an efficient energy supply. Once this is achieved, the focus can then shift to ensuring that the supplied energy is renewable to the greatest extent possible. It is therefore important to first optimize the building’s performance, and then consider the energy supply system and energy source so that the building is also supplied with as little primary energy and as much renewable and environmentally friendly energy as possible. The Swedish Energy Agency’s strategy proposes an energy requirement—as a target for nearly zero-energy buildings—equal to half of the current energy requirements in the Swedish National Board of Housing, Building and Planning’s building regulations. The results show that this would entail fairly high requirements for small buildings in the far north. An example of measures that would enable this level to be achieved even for a single-family home in Kiruna includes the elimination of thermal bridges, heat recovery from ventilation air with 90% efficiency, windows with a U-value of 0.7 W/m²K, doors with 0.6 W/m²K, airtightness of 0.2 l/s, m², and a window-to-wall ratio of 10%. This is at the upper limit of what is possible to achieve with current technology. Heat exchangers with 90% efficiency are available today, but since heat exchangers need to be defrosted in cold climates—which lowers their actual efficiency—it may be difficult to achieve this efficiency in practice in Kiruna.  However, given future technological developments and greater experience with low-energy construction, the Energy Agency’s proposal represents a reasonable standard for nearly zero-energy buildings throughout the country. It would, however, be advantageous to divide the country into more climate zones—or at least a more even division—since the variation in energy use for similar buildings is currently significantly greater in the northernmost climate zone than in the southernmost. In the southern parts of Sweden, it would be possible to set the passive house standard as the requirement for nearly zero-energy buildings, but this is not recommended in the northern parts, as small buildings in particular are currently very difficult to construct to the passive house standard in cold climates. Achieving the passive house standard for single-family homes in the northernmost part of the country could require an indoor temperature not exceeding 20°C and an airtightness of 0. l/s·m². This is not reasonable, primarily because, according to user data, higher indoor temperatures are common in Sweden and because such a high level of airtightness cannot be guaranteed today. The area in Västra Sörhaga, Alingsås, on the other hand, is a realistic candidate for construction to the passive house standard, as it is located in climate zone III and would then almost certainly also meet the standard for nearly zero-energy buildings. If the area were built to passive house standards, this would result in a total specific energy consumption of approximately 50 kWh/m² per year, and a total energy consumption equivalent to 45% of the energy consumption of a similar area that meets the current energy requirements in the Swedish National Board of Housing, Building and Planning’s building regulations. Approximately 180,000 kWh/year could be supplied by district heating. The Swedish National Board of Housing, Building and Planning’s 2011 proposal for revising the energy requirements is not considered sufficiently ambitious and makes it very difficult to achieve the environmental goals by 2020. Further studies are needed on the economic viability of low-energy buildings, and to evaluate the supplied energy in a consistent manner, primary energy factors must be developed. These should include production efficiency, energy form, and energy source. Energy sources, in turn, should be assessed based on their degree of renewability, availability, and storage potential. Low-energy construction requires a major transformation of the existing construction market and energy structure. To meet this goal by 2021, we must start now! title: Significance of the Climate and Energy Package for the Development of Renewable Energy Sources in the European Union
abstract: The Climate and Energy Package obliges member states to pursue a common aim - to increase the share of renewable energy to 20% in the general energy balance of the European Union by 2020. This is a challenging task, since the renewable energy sector requires significant financial support to increase its competitiveness, compared to traditional energy sources. When adopting the Energy Package, leaders of member states did not anticipate the economic crisis and its impact on the European Union’s economy, and on the energy sector in particular.Pakiet energetyczno- klimatyczny zobligował kraje członkowskie do spełnienia wspólnego celu jakim jest zwiększenie udziału energii z odnawialnych źródeł energii do 20% w ogólnym bilansie energetycznym UE do 2020.r Jest to zadanie niezwykle trudne gdyż sektor odnawialnych źródeł wymaga rozwoju i ogromnego wsparcia finansowego, w celu zwiększenia jego konkurencyjności w stosunku do źródeł konwencjonalnych energii. Przyjmując pakiet energetyczny przywódcy państw członkowskich nie przewidzieli kryzysu gospodarczego i jego wpływu na gospodarkę Unii Europejskiej, w tym na sektor energetyczny
title -English: Significance of the Climate and Energy Package for the Development of Renewable Energy Sources in the European Union
abstract - English: The Climate and Energy Package obliges member states to pursue a common aim - to increase the share of renewable energy to 20% in the general energy balance of the European Union by 2020. This is a challenging task, since the renewable energy sector requires significant financial support to increase its competitiveness, compared to traditional energy sources. When adopting the Energy Package, leaders of member states did not anticipate the economic crisis and its impact on the European Union’s economy, and on the energy sector in particular.   translation: The Energy and Climate Package committed member states to meeting a common goal of increasing the share of energy from renewable sources to 20% of the EU’s total energy mix by 2020. This is an extremely difficult task, as the renewable energy sector requires development and substantial financial support, in order to increase its competitiveness relative to conventional energy sources. When adopting the energy package, the leaders of the member states did not foresee the economic crisis and its impact on the European Union’s economy, including the energy sector.

title: Testing of Slovakian quasigeoids on the new national realization of the EVRS height systém
abstract:
Based on the Decree No. 300/2009 Z.z. of the Office of Geodesy, Cartography and Cadastre of the Slovak Republic, (hereinafter referred to as the "Decree"), two height systems are binding on the territory of Slovakia: the Baltic Height System after adjustment (Bpv) with the code EPSG: 8357 and the European Vertical Reference System (EVRS). The national implementation of the EVRS height system in Slovakia is SKVRF2024, which corresponds to EVRF2019 with the EPSG code: 9390. To convert between ellipsoidal heights in ETRS89 and normal heights in the Bpv, the decree provides for the use of the digital height reference model DVRM05 [3].
Simultaneously with the calculation of the SKVRF2024 realization, a new gravimetric model of the Slovak quasi-geoid called GMSQ2019 [2] with a spatial resolution of 2'' (~ 60 m) was acquired by the the Geodetic and Cartographic Institute in Bratislava (GKÚ). In 2024, this version of the quasi-geoid was recalculated by the author using the latest digital relief model DMR5.0 and two new versions of the quasi-geoid with a spatial resolution of 1'' (~ 30 m) were created with the names GMSQ2024A and GMSQ2024B [1].
The presentation focuses on the comparison of normal heights in the SKVRF2024 realization with normal heights calculated from ellipsoids using different quasi-geoids. The following quasi-geoids of Slovakia were used to transform the ellipsoidal heights: GMSQ2019, GMSQ2024A, GMSQ2024B and DVRM05. This comparison was carried out on two sets of points of the National Spatial Network (ŠPS), namely on the points of the permanent SKPOS stations (36 points), where the accuracy of the ellipsoid heights is at the level of 10 mm, and on a set of class B and C points (851 points), where the accuracy of the ellipsoid heights is up to 25 mm. The points used for fitting the DVRM05 were not included in this set.
The following table shows the differences of the normal heights of the SKVRF2024 with the ellipsoidal heights calculated using different quasi-geoids. In the comparison of the quasi-geoids on the SKPOS points, the GMSQ2024A quasi-geoid had the lowest mean error. However, the differences in standard deviations compared to quasi-geoids GMSQ2024B and GMSQ2019 were minimal and only differed at the 1mm level. Similar results, but with a higher mean error, were also observed when using a more extensive test point set, the class B and C point, which have lower quality ellipsoidal heights. However, the accuracy of this set of points is much closer to the real accuracy of ellipsoidal heights determined in normal surveying practice.
Based on the obtained results, it can be argued that all three latest quasi-geoids GMSQ2019, GMSQ2024A and GMSQ2024B performed better in combination with the heights of the SKVRF2024 than with using the older quasi-geoid DVRM05, which was fitted to Bpv57. It should also be added that the results presented were obtained without fitting the new GMSQ20XX quasi-geoids. On the basis of this comparison, the GMSQ2024A quasi-geoid will be subject to subsequent fitting at the GKÚ. It performed best when tested and was calculated using the latest data currently available to the ÚGKK. After the fitting, the GMSQ2024A quasi-geoid will ensure that surveyors can determine normal heights in the SKVRF2024 realization using GNSS instruments with better accuracy than it is currently in the case using Bpv and DVRM05.Na základe vyhlášky č. 300/2009 Z. z. Úradu geodézie, kartografie a katastra Slovenskej republiky, ktorou sa vykonáva zákon Národnej rady Slovenskej republiky č. 215/1995 Z. z. o geodézii a kartografii v znení neskorších predpisov (ďalej ako „Vyhláška“) sú na území Slovenska záväzné dva výškové systémy: Baltský výškový systém po vyrovnaní (Bpv) s kódom EPSG: 8357 a Európsky vertikálny referenčný systém (EVRS). Národnou realizáciou výškového systému EVRS na Slovensku je SKVRF2024, ktorá zodpovedá EVRF2019 s kódom EPSG: 9390. Na prevod medzi elipsoidickými výškami v systéme ETRS89 a normálnymi výškami v realizácii Bpv vyhláška stanovuje použitie digitálneho výškového referenčného modelu DVRM05 [3].
Súčasne s výpočtom realizácie SKVRF2024 bol správcom geodetických základov na Slovensku, ktorým je Geodetický a kartografický ústav Bratislava (GKÚ), obstaraný aj nový gravimetrický model slovenského kvázigeoidu s označením GMSQ2019 [2] s priestorovým rozlíšením 2´´ (~ 60 m). Na reprezentáciu terénu bol použitý digitálny model reliéfu DMR3.5. V roku 2024 bola táto verzia kvázigeoidu autorom prepočítaná, pričom bol použitý najaktuálnejší digitálny model reliéfu DMR5.0 a vznikli dve nové verzie kvázigeoidu s priestorovým rozlíšením 1´´ (~ 30 m) pod označením GMSQ2024A a GMSQ2024B [1]. Porovnaním týchto verzií kvázigoidov s pôvodnou GMSQ2019 boli autorom deklarované rozdiely ~0.08 m, a to najvýraznejšie v exponovanom teréne [1].
Príspevok je zameraný na porovnanie normálnych výšok v národnej realizácii SKVRF2024 s normálnymi výškami vypočítanými z elipsoidických použitím rôznych kvázgeoidov. Na transformáciu elipsoidických výšok boli použité nasledovné kvázigeoidy Slovenska: GMSQ2019, GMSQ2024A, GMSQ2024B a DVRM05. Toto porovnanie bolo realizované na dvoch množinách bodov Štátnej priestorovej siete (ŠPS), a to na bodoch permanentných staníc SKPOS (36 bodov), kde presnosť elipsoidických výšok je na úrovni 10 mm a na množine bodov triedy B a C (851 bodov), kde presnosť elipsoidickej výšky je do 25 mm. Z tejto množiny boli vylúčené body, ktoré slúžili na fitovanie DVRM05. Porovnaním kvázigeoidov na bodoch SKPOS bola najnižšia stredná chyba dosiahnutá pri kvázigeoide GMSQ2024A. Rozdiely stredných chýb oproti kvázigeoidom GMSQ2024B a GMSQ2019 boli však minimálne a líšili sa iba na úrovni 1 mm. Podobné výsledky, avšak s vyššou hodnotou strednej chyby, boli zaznamenané aj pri použití rozsiahlejšej testovacej množiny bodov, a to množiny bodov ŠPS triedy B a C, ktorá má nižšiu kvalitu elipsoidických výšok. Táto množina bodov sa však svojou presnosťou oveľa viac približuje k reálnej presnosti elipsoidických výšok určovaných v bežnej geodetickej praxi.
Na základe dosiahnutých výsledkov možno tvrdiť, že všetky tri najnovšie kvázigeoidy GMSQ2019, GMSQ2024A a GMSQ2024B dosahovali v kombinácii s výškami novej realizácie výškového systému SKVRF2024 lepšie výsledky, ako v prípade použitia staršieho kvázigeoidu DVRM05, ktorý bol nafitovaný na Bpv57. Je taktiež potrebné dodať, že odprezentované výsledky boli dosiahnuté bez akéhokoľvek fitovania nových kvázigeoidov GMSQ20XX. Je zrejmé, že ich nafitovaním by boli dosiahnuté lepšie výsledky. Na základe tohto porovnania bude predmetom následného fitovania na GKÚ kvázigeoid GMSQ2024A, nakoľko vyšiel z testovania najlepšie a na jeho výpočet boli použité najnovšie dostupné dáta, ktorými v súčasnosti ÚGKK disponuje. Kvázigeoid GMSQ2024A po nafitovaní zaručí geodetom v praxi určovať normálne výšky v realizácii SKVRF2024 prístrojmi GNSS s lepšou presnosťou ako je tomu v súčasnosti pri využívaní Bpv a DVRM05
title -English: Testing of Slovakian quasigeoids on the new national realization of the EVRS height system
abstract - English:
Based on the Decree No. 300/2009 Z.z. of the Office of Geodesy, Cartography and Cadastre of the Slovak Republic, (hereinafter referred to as the "Decree"), two height systems are binding on the territory of Slovakia: the Baltic Height System after adjustment (Bpv) with the code EPSG: 8357 and the European Vertical Reference System (EVRS). The national implementation of the EVRS height system in Slovakia is SKVRF2024, which corresponds to EVRF2019 with the EPSG code: 9390. To convert between ellipsoidal heights in ETRS89 and normal heights in the Bpv, the decree provides for the use of the digital height reference model DVRM05 [3].
Simultaneously with the calculation of the SKVRF2024 realization, a new gravimetric model of the Slovak quasi-geoid called GMSQ2019 [2] with a spatial resolution of 2'' (~ 60 m) was acquired by the the Geodetic and Cartographic Institute in Bratislava (GKÚ). In 2024, this version of the quasi-geoid was recalculated by the author using the latest digital relief model DMR5.0 and two new versions of the quasi-geoid with a spatial resolution of 1'' (~ 30 m) were created with the names GMSQ2024A and GMSQ2024B [1].
The presentation focuses on the comparison of normal heights in the SKVRF2024 realization with normal heights calculated from ellipsoids using different quasi-geoids. The following quasi-geoids of Slovakia were used to transform the ellipsoidal heights: GMSQ2019, GMSQ2024A, GMSQ2024B and DVRM05. This comparison was carried out on two sets of points of the National Spatial Network (ŠPS), namely on the points of the permanent SKPOS stations (36 points), where the accuracy of the ellipsoid heights is at the level of 10 mm, and on a set of class B and C points (851 points), where the accuracy of the ellipsoid heights is up to 25 mm. The points used for fitting the DVRM05 were not included in this set.
The following table shows the differences of the normal heights of the SKVRF2024 with the ellipsoidal heights calculated using different quasi-geoids. In the comparison of the quasi-geoids on the SKPOS points, the GMSQ2024A quasi-geoid had the lowest mean error. However, the differences in standard deviations compared to quasi-geoids GMSQ2024B and GMSQ2019 were minimal and only differed at the 1mm level. Similar results, but with a higher mean error, were also observed when using a more extensive test point set, the class B and C point, which have lower quality ellipsoidal heights. However, the accuracy of this set of points is much closer to the real accuracy of ellipsoidal heights determined in normal surveying practice.
Based on the obtained results, it can be argued that all three latest quasi-geoids GMSQ2019, GMSQ2024A and GMSQ2024B performed better in combination with the heights of the SKVRF2024 than with using the older quasi-geoid DVRM05, which was fitted to Bpv57. It should also be added that the results presented were obtained without fitting the new GMSQ20XX quasi-geoids. On the basis of this comparison, the GMSQ2024A quasi-geoid will be subject to subsequent fitting at the GKÚ. It performed best when tested and was calculated using the latest data currently available to the ÚGKK. After the fitting, the GMSQ2024A quasi-geoid will ensure that surveyors can determine normal heights in the SKVRF2024 realization using GNSS instruments with better accuracy than it is currently in the case using Bpv and DVRM05.   translation: Pursuant to Decree No. 300/2009 Z. z. of the Office of Geodesy, Cartography, and Cadastre of the Slovak Republic, implementing Act No. 215/1995 Coll. of the National Council of the Slovak Republic on Geodesy and Cartography, as amended (hereinafter referred to as the “Decree”), two height systems are binding on the territory of Slovakia: the Baltic Height System after adjustment (Bpv) with EPSG code: 8357, and the European Vertical Reference System (EVRS). The national implementation of the EVRS height system in Slovakia is SKVRF2024, which corresponds to EVRF2019 with EPSG code: 9390. For the conversion between ellipsoidal heights in the ETRS89 system and normal heights in the Bpv implementation, the decree stipulates the use of the digital height reference model DVRM05 [3].

Concurrently with the calculation of the SKVRF2024 realization, the administrator of geodetic infrastructure in Slovakia—the Geodetic and Cartographic Institute in Bratislava (GKÚ)—also procured a new gravimetric model of the Slovak quasi-geoid designated GMSQ2019 [2] with a spatial resolution of 2″ (~60 m). The DMR3.5 digital elevation model was used to represent the terrain. In 2024, the author recalculated this version of the quasi-geoid using the most up-to-date digital elevation model, DMR5.0, resulting in two new versions of the quasi-geoid with a spatial resolution of 1'' (~30 m), designated GMSQ2024A and GMSQ2024B [1]. By comparing these versions of the quasi-geoid with the original GMSQ2019, the author reported differences of ~0.08 m, most pronounced in exposed terrain [1].

This paper focuses on comparing normal heights in  the national implementation of SKVRF2024 with normal heights calculated from ellipsoidal heights using various quasi-geoids. The following Slovak quasi-geoids were used to transform the ellipsoidal heights: GMSQ2019, GMSQ2024A, GMSQ2024B, and DVRM05. This comparison was performed on two sets of points from the State Spatial Network (ŠPS): points from SKPOS permanent stations (36 points), where the accuracy of ellipsoidal heights is 10 mm, and on a set of Class B and C points (851 points), where the accuracy of ellipsoidal heights is within 25 mm. Points used for fitting the DVRM05 were excluded from this set. By comparing quasi-geoids at SKPOS points, the lowest mean error was achieved with the GMSQ2024A quasi-geoid. However, the differences in mean errors compared to the GMSQ2024B and GMSQ2019 quasi-geoids were minimal, differing by only 1 mm. Similar results, albeit with a higher mean error, were also observed when using a more extensive test dataset—specifically, the set of ŠPS Class B and C points, which has lower-quality ellipsoidal heights. However, in terms of accuracy, this point set is much closer to the actual accuracy of ellipsoidal heights determined in standard geodetic practice.

Based on the results obtained, it can be concluded that all three of the latest quasi-geoids—GMSQ2019, GMSQ2024A, and GMSQ2024B, in combination with the heights of the new SKVRF2024 height system, achieved better results than when using the older DVRM05 quasi-geoid, which was fitted to Bpv57.  It should also be noted that the presented results were obtained without any fitting of the new GMSQ20XX quasi-geoids. It is clear that better results would have been achieved by fitting them. Based on this comparison, the GMSQ2024A quasi-geoid will be the subject of subsequent fitting at the GKÚ, as it performed best in testing and was calculated using the latest available data currently held by the ÚGKK. Once fitted, the GMSQ2024A quasi-geoid will enable surveyors to determine normal heights in the SKVRF2024 system using GNSS instruments with greater accuracy than is currently possible when using Bpv and DVRM05.

title: How do we calculate coordinates of the reference stations within SKPOS?
abstract: GKÚ Bratislava is the operator of the Slovak real-time positioning service (SKPOS). In addition to the operation of the control software, user management, quality monitoring and other interesting activities, the National Service Centre of SKPOS is also dedicated to the management of GNSS reference stations, including the calculation of their coordinates. The number of SKPOS reference stations with calculated spatial coordinates and velocities directly represents the national ETRS89 realization in Slovakia. Therefore, the reliability of the calculated coordinates also has a direct impact on the accuracy of the national reference frame and the reliability of positioning in the ETRF2000 within SKPOS. The paper describes in detail the procedure of calculating the official coordinates of SKPOS reference stations using Bernese GNSS software, through their verification up to their acceptance by the EUREF Executive Committee. It also describes how the SKPOS National Service Center calculates the coordinates of new SKPOS stations after their establishment or after GNSS antenna replacement.GKÚ Bratislava je prevádzkovateľom Slovenskej priestorovej observačnej služby (SKPOS). Národné servisné centrum SKPOS sa okrem obsluhy riadiaceho softvéru, správy používateľov, monitoringu kvality a ďalších zaujímavých aktivít venuje aj správe referenčných staníc GNSS, vrátane výpočtu ich súradníc. Množina referenčných staníc SKPOS s vypočítanými priestorovými súradnicami, resp. ich rýchlosťami priamo reprezentuje národnú realizáciu systému ETRS89 na Slovensku. Z tohto dôvodu má spoľahlivosť vypočítaných súradníc priamy vplyv aj na presnosť referenčného rámca a spoľahlivosť určovania polohy v platnej národnej realizácii v rámci SKPOS. Príspevok podrobnejšie opisuje postup výpočtu oficiálnych súradníc referenčných staníc SKPOS v systéme ETRS89 pomocou softvéru Bernese, cez ich verifikáciu až po akceptáciu výkonným výborom EUREF. Okrem toho príspevok opisuje, akým spôsobom národné servisné centrum SKPOS pristupuje k výpočtu súradníc nových staníc SKPOS krátko po ich zriadení, resp. po výmene antén GNSS
title -English: How do we calculate coordinates of the reference stations within SKPOS?
abstract - English: GKÚ Bratislava is the operator of the Slovak real-time positioning service (SKPOS). In addition to the operation of the control software, user management, quality monitoring and other interesting activities, the National Service Centre of SKPOS is also dedicated to the management of GNSS reference stations, including the calculation of their coordinates. The number of SKPOS reference stations with calculated spatial coordinates and velocities directly represents the national ETRS89 realization in Slovakia. Therefore, the reliability of the calculated coordinates also has a direct impact on the accuracy of the national reference frame and the reliability of positioning in the ETRF2000 within SKPOS. The paper describes in detail the procedure of calculating the official coordinates of SKPOS reference stations using Bernese GNSS software, through their verification up to their acceptance by the EUREF Executive Committee. It also describes how the SKPOS National Service Center calculates the coordinates of new SKPOS stations after their establishment or after GNSS antenna replacement.  GKÚ Bratislava operates the Slovak Spatial Observation Service (SKPOS). In addition to operating the control software, managing users, monitoring quality, and other related activities, the SKPOS National Service Center is also responsible for managing GNSS reference stations, including the calculation of their coordinates. The set of SKPOS reference stations with calculated spatial coordinates and their velocities directly represents the national implementation of the ETRS89 system in Slovakia. For this reason, the reliability of the calculated coordinates directly affects the accuracy of the reference frame and the reliability of position determination within the current national implementation of SKPOS. This paper describes in detail the procedure for calculating the official coordinates of SKPOS reference stations in the ETRS89 system using Bernese software, from their verification through to their acceptance by the EUREF Executive Committee. In addition, the paper describes how the SKPOS National Service Center calculates the coordinates of new SKPOS stations shortly after their establishment or after the replacement of GNSS antennas.

title: The Development of the Discipline of Conservation and Restoration of Paper, Bookbinding, and Documents in Slovakia
abstract:
Diplomová práca sa zameriava na vývoj odboru konzervovania a reštaurovania papiera, knižnej väzby a dokumentov na Slovensku. Cieľom práce je zmapovať vývoj reštaurátorských techník, ako aj formovanie inštitucionálneho zázemia v dielňach archívov a knižníc.
Spracovanie údajov vychádza najmä z prieskumu reštaurovaných objektov pochádzajúcich z fondov Slovenského národného archívu, Univerzitnej knižnice v Bratislave, Slovenskej národnej knižnice a ďalších inštitúcií. Práca zároveň reflektuje legislatívne rámce, odborné štandardy, profesijný vývoj reštaurátorov a etické aspekty zásahov. Pozornosť je venovaná aj analýze materiálového vybavenia pracovísk a dokumentácii reštaurátorských zásahov. Záverečná časť sa venuje analýze zásahov na vybraných gotických polousňových väzbách z fondu Knižnice Bratislavskej kapituly v Slovenskom národnom archíve, pričom na tento chronologický vývoj prístupov nadväzuje reštaurovanie konvolútu tlačí so sign. Phil. 30, prevedené v rámci tejto diplomovej práce.The thesis focuses on the development of the field of conservation and restoration of paper, bookbinding, and documents in Slovakia. The thesis aims to map the development of restoration techniques, as well as the formation of the institutional background in archive and library workshops.
The data processing is mainly based on a survey of restored objects from the funds of the Slovak National Archive, the University Library in Bratislava, the Slovak National Library, and other institutions. The thesis also reflects legislative frameworks, professional standards, the professional development of restorers, and the ethical aspects of interventions. Attention is also paid to the analysis of the material equipment of the workplaces and the documentation of restoration interventions. The final part is devoted to the study of interventions on selected Gothic half-leather bindings from the fund of the Bratislava Chapter Library in the Slovak National Archives, while this chronological development of approaches is followed by the restoration of the convolute printed with the sign. title -English: The Development of the Discipline of Conservation and Restoration of Paper, Bookbinding, and Documents in Slovakia
abstract - English:
This thesis focuses on the development of the field of conservation and restoration of paper, bookbindings, and documents in Slovakia. The aim of the thesis is to chart the evolution of restoration techniques, as well as the development of the institutional infrastructure in the workshops of archives and libraries.
The data analysis is based primarily on a survey of restored items from the collections of the Slovak National Archives, the University Library in Bratislava, the Slovak National Library, and other institutions. The thesis also examines legislative frameworks, professional standards, the professional development of conservators, and the ethical aspects of conservation interventions. Attention is also given to the analysis of the material resources available in conservation workshops and the documentation of conservation interventions. The concluding section analyzes interventions on selected Gothic half-leather-bound volumes from the collection of the Bratislava Chapter Library at the Slovak National Archives; this chronological development of approaches is followed by the restoration of a collection of printed works with the call number Phil. 30, carried out as part of this master’s thesis. title: Analysis of the determinants of price differences in the real estate market in Bratislava and its surroundings
abstract: This thesis examines the determinants of regional differences in property prices in Slovakia, with a focus on the Bratislava region, over the period 2005–2023. Using data from the Statistical Office of the Slovak Republic (Datacube), we conducted regression analyses and a panel model with both fixed and random effects to identify the impact of macroeconomic indicators (GDP per capita, unemployment rate, economic activity, interest rates), demographic variables (number of marriages), and supply factors (number of completed apartments) on the average apartment price per square meter. All quantitative analyses are conducted in program R.Táto diplomová práca skúma determinanty regionálnych rozdielov v cenách nehnuteľností na Slovensku, so zameraním na Bratislavský kraj, v období rokov 2005– 2023. S využitím údajov zo Štatistického úradu SR (Datacube) sme realizovali regresné analýzy a panelový model s fixnými aj náhodnými efektmi, aby sme identifikovali vplyv makroekonomických ukazovateľov (HDP na obyvateľa, miera nezamestnanosti, ekonomická aktivita, úrokové sadzby), demografických premenných (počet uzavretých manželstiev) a faktorov ponuky (počet dokončených bytov) na priemernú cenu bytov za m². Kvantitatívne analýzy boli realizované v prostredí R.Tato diplomová práce zkoumá determinanty regionálních rozdílů v cenách nemovitostí na Slovensku, se zaměřením na Bratislavský kraj, v období let 2005–2023. S využitím údajů ze Statistického úřadu SR (Datacube) jsme realizovali regresní analýzy a panelový model s fixními i náhodnými efekty, abychom identifikovali vliv makroekonomických ukazatelů (HDP na obyvatele, míra nezaměstnanosti, ekonomická aktivita, úrokové sazby), demografických proměnných (počet uzavřených manželství) a faktorů nabídky (počet dokončených bytů) na průměrnou cenu bytů za m². Kvantitativní analýzy byly provedeny v prostředí R

title: Contemporary Hungarian Theatre in Southwestern Slovakia
abstract: Tato bakalářská práce zkoumá současný stav divadelnictví v maďarské menšině na jihozápadním Slovensku. Hlavní část výzkumu se zaměřuje na případové studie tří klíčových institucí: divadla Jókai v Komárnu, Tanečního divadla Ifjú Szivek a nezávislé divadelní platformy Trepp. Práce se zaměřuje na kulturní, organizační a finanční podmínky ovlivňující provoz těchto institucí a snaží se identifikovat současné výzvy, příležitosti a omezení, které formují jejich aktivity. Výzkum také reflektuje vztah mezi menšinovou a majoritní (slovenskou) divadelní scénou a zkoumá umělecký potenciál, který nabízí dvojjazyčnost. Pro poskytnutí širšího kontextu se věnuje také maďarské divadelní činnosti v oblasti mezi Bratislavou a Komárnem. Studie využívá kombinaci kvalitativních metod, včetně polostrukturovaných rozhovorů s klíčovými zástupci vybraných divadel a analýzy relevantních dokumentů.This Bachelor's thesis examines the current state of theatre within the Hungarian minority in southwestern Slovakia. The core of the research consists of case studies of three key institutions: the Jókai Theatre in Komárno, the Ifjú Szivek Dance Theatre, and the independent theatre platform Trepp. The thesis focuses on the cultural, organizational, and financial conditions influencing the operation of these institutions and aims to identify the current challenges, opportunities, and limitations shaping their activities. The research also reflects on the relationship between the minority and majority (Slovak) theatre scenes and explores the artistic potential offered by bilingualism. To provide broader context, attention is also given to Hungarian theatrical activity in the area between Bratislava and Komárno. The study relies on a combination of qualitative methods, including semi-structured interviews with key representatives of the selected theatres and analysis of relevant documents.Bakalárska práca sa zaoberá súčasným stavom divadelníctva maďarskej národnostnej menšiny na juhozápadnom Slovensku. Hlavným predmetom sú prípadové štúdie troch kľúčových subjektov: Jókaiho divadla v Komárne, Tanečného divadla Ifjú Szivek a divadelnej platformy Trepp. Práca sa zameriava na kultúrne, organizačné a finančné podmienky, ktoré ovplyvňujú fungovanie týchto inštitúcií, a usiluje sa identifikovať aktuálne výzvy, príležitosti a obmedzenia, ktoré formujú ich činnosť. Súčasťou výskumu je aj reflexia vzťahu medzi menšinovou a väčšinovou (slovenskou) divadelnou scénou, ako aj skúmanie možností, ktoré prináša dvojjazyčnosť v umeleckej tvorbe. Pre širší kontext je pozornosť venovaná aj maďarskej divadelnej činnosti v oblasti medzi Bratislavou a Komárnom. Výskum je realizovaný kombináciou kvalitatívnych metód, prostredníctvom pološtruktúrovaných rozhovorov s kľúčovými predstaviteľmi jednotlivých divadiel a analýzy dostupných dokumentov
title -English: Contemporary Hungarian Theatre in Southwestern Slovakia
abstract - English: This bachelor’s thesis examines the current state of theater within the Hungarian minority in southwestern Slovakia. The main part of the research focuses on case studies of three key institutions: the Jókai Theater in Komárno, the Ifjú Szivek Dance Theater, and the independent theater platform Trepp. The thesis examines the cultural, organizational, and financial conditions affecting the operation of these institutions and seeks to identify the current challenges, opportunities, and constraints that shape their activities. The research also reflects on the relationship between the minority and majority (Slovak) theater scenes and explores the artistic potential offered by bilingualism. To provide a broader context, it also examines Hungarian theater activities in the region between Bratislava and Komárno. The study employs a combination of qualitative methods, including semi-structured interviews with key representatives of the selected theaters and an analysis of relevant documents. The research also reflects on the relationship between the minority and majority (Slovak) theatre scenes and explores the artistic potential offered by bilingualism. To provide broader context, attention is also given to Hungarian theatrical activity in the area between Bratislava and Komárno. The study relies on a combination of qualitative methods, including semi-structured interviews with key representatives of the selected theatres and analysis of relevant documents. This bachelor’s thesis examines the current state of theater within the Hungarian national minority in southwestern Slovakia. It focuses primarily on case studies of three key institutions: the Jókai Theater in Komárno, the Ifjú Szivek Dance Theater, and the Trepp theater platform. The thesis focuses on the cultural, organizational, and financial conditions that influence the functioning of these institutions and seeks to identify the current challenges, opportunities, and limitations that shape their activities. The research also includes a reflection on the relationship between the minority and majority (Slovak) theater scenes, as well as an exploration of the possibilities that bilingualism offers in artistic creation. To provide a broader context, attention is also given to Hungarian theater activities in the region between Bratislava and Komárno. The research is conducted using a combination of qualitative methods, including semi-structured interviews with key representatives of individual theaters and an analysis of available documents.

title: Niezaangażowany obserwator? Słowacja wobec Inicjatywy Trójmorza (2015-2024)
abstract: The Three Seas Initiative (3SI) is a relatively new format of regional cooperation in Central Europe focused on economic cooperation. From the beginning, Slovakia has been a member of the initiative, but it is one of the least involved participating countries in the Three Seas cooperation. This is due to many international and domestic factors. Bratislava, above all, does not see any added value in the 3SI and focuses primarily on EU funds in its regional development. The format is also perceived as aimed at EU unity and a counterweight to German dominance. The new US administration of Donald Trump and its approach to the 3SI provides an opportunity to change the position of the Slovak authorities. The current president of Slovakia, Peter Pellegrini, may pursue a similarly new policy towards the format. The article presents the conditions for Slovakia's functioning in the 3SI in the years 2015-2024, state and expert entities involved in the Three Seas cooperation, public discourse on the 3SI and the most important projects involving the Slovak side. An attempt was also made to answer the question about the reasons for Slovakia's distancing itself from this format of regional cooperation and the possibilities of overcoming this reluctance.Inicjatywa Trójmorza (Three Seas Initiative, 3SI) to stosunkowo nowy format współpracy regionalnej w Europie Środkowej skoncentrowany na współpracy ekonomicznej. Od początku w jego skład wchodzi Słowacja, która zalicza się jednak do grupy najmniej zaangażowanych we współpracę trójmorską państw uczestniczących. Jest to uwarunkowane wieloma czynnikami międzynarodowymi i wewnątrzkrajowymi. Bratysława przede wszystkim nie widzi w 3SI żadnej wartości dodanej i stawia w swoim rozwoju regionalnym przede wszystkim na fundusze UE. Format jest także postrzegany jako wymierzony w jedność UE i przeciwwagę dla dominacji Niemiec. Szansę na zmianę stanowiska władz słowackich stanowi nowa administracja amerykańska Donalda Trumpa i jej podejście do 3SI. Podobnie nową politykę wobec formatu może prowadzić obecny prezydent Słowacji Peter Pellegrini. W artykule przedstawione zostały uwarunkowania funkcjonowania Słowacji w 3SI w latach 2015-2024, podmioty państwowe i eksperckie zajmujące się współpracą trójmorską, dyskurs publiczny o 3SI i najważniejsze projekty z udziałem strony słowackiej. Podjęta została również próba odpowiedzi na pytanie o przyczyny dystansowania się Słowacji od tego formatu współpracy regionalnej i możliwości przełamania tej niechęci
title -English: An Uninvolved Observer? Slovakia and the Three Seas Initiative (2015–2024)
abstract - English: The Three Seas Initiative (3SI) is a relatively new format of regional cooperation in Central Europe focused on economic cooperation. From the beginning, Slovakia has been a member of the initiative, but it is one of the least involved participating countries in the Three Seas cooperation. This is due to many international and domestic factors. Bratislava, above all, does not see any added value in the 3SI and focuses primarily on EU funds in its regional development. The current president of Slovakia, Peter Pellegrini, may pursue a similarly new policy towards the format. The article presents the conditions for Slovakia's functioning in the 3SI in the years 2015-2024, state and expert entities involved in the Three Seas cooperation, public discourse on the 3SI and the most important projects involving the Slovak side. An attempt was also made to answer the question about the reasons for Slovakia's distancing itself from this format of regional cooperation and the possibilities of overcoming this reluctance.  The Three Seas Initiative (3SI) is a relatively new regional cooperation framework in Central Europe focused on economic cooperation. Slovakia has been a member from the outset; however, it ranks among the least engaged of the participating countries in Three Seas cooperation. This is due to a number of international and domestic factors. Above all, Bratislava does not see any added value in the 3SI and relies primarily on EU funds for its regional development. The initiative is also perceived as aimed at undermining EU unity and serving as a counterweight to German dominance. The new U.S. administration under Donald Trump and its approach to the 3SI present an opportunity for a shift in the Slovak government’s stance. Similarly, Slovakia’s current Prime Minister, Peter Pellegrini, may pursue a new policy toward the initiative. This article examines the conditions governing Slovakia’s participation in the 3SI from 2015 to 2024, the government and expert entities involved in Three Seas cooperation, the public discourse on the 3SI, and the most important projects involving Slovakia. An attempt is also made to answer the question of why Slovakia has distanced itself from this regional cooperation framework and how this reluctance might be overcome.

title: Penerapan Layanan Konseling Individual dengan Menggunakan Pendekatan Gestalt Untuk Mengurangi Stres Akademik Siswa di SMKN 1 Percut Sei Tuan
abstract:
This study was conducted by researchers to address the issue of academic stress experienced by students at SMKN 1 Percut Sei Tuan. Academic stress is a state of pressure arising from school demands, such as numerous assignments and high expectations from teachers or the environment. This pressure can affect students' behavior in learning and impact both their academic performance and mental health.
The purpose of this research is to reduce students' academic stress by implementing individual counseling services using the Gestalt approach. The Gestalt approach was chosen because it focuses on students' self-awareness, which is expected to help them view problems holistically and develop responsibility in addressing these issues.
This study is a qualitative research with a descriptive approach, involving three students as subjects. Data were collected through observations and interviews to identify the factors of academic stress experienced by students and to evaluate the effectiveness of the counseling provided. Data analysis was carried out through reduction, data presentation, and conclusion drawing.
The research findings indicate that individual counseling services using the Gestalt approach significantly reduce students' academic stress levels. Before counseling, students exhibited high-stress symptoms that affected their behavior and academic outcomes. After receiving counseling, they became more capable of managing their time and meeting academic demands more effectively.
Penelitian ini dilakukan oleh peneliti untuk mengatasi permasalahan stres akademik yang dialami siswa di SMKN 1 Percut Sei Tuan. Stres akademik adalah kondisi tekanan yang muncul akibat tuntutan sekolah, seperti banyaknya tugas dan ekspektasi yang tinggi dari guru atau lingkungan. Tekanan ini dapat memengaruhi perilaku siswa dalam belajar dan berdampak pada hasil akademik serta kesehatan mental mereka.
Penelitian ini bertujuan mengurangi stres akademik siswa dengan menerapkan layanan konseling individual menggunakan pendekatan Gestalt. Pendekatan Gestalt dipilih karena berfokus pada kesadaran diri siswa, yang diharapkan dapat membantu mereka melihat masalah secara menyeluruh dan mengembangkan tanggung jawab untuk mengatasi permasalahan tersebut.
Penelitian ini merupakan penelitian kualitatif dengan pendekatan deskriptif, melibatkan tiga siswa sebagai subjek. Data diperoleh melalui observasi dan wawancara untuk mengidentifikasi faktor-faktor stres akademik yang dialami siswa dan untuk mengevaluasi efektivitas konseling yang diberikan. Analisis data dilakukan melalui proses reduksi, penyajian data, dan penarikan kesimpulan.
Hasil penelitian menunjukkan bahwa layanan konseling individual dengan pendekatan Gestalt mampu mengurangi tingkat stres akademik siswa secara signifikan. Sebelum layanan konseling, siswa menunjukkan gejala stres tinggi yang berdampak pada perilaku dan hasil belajar. Setelah menerima konseling, mereka mulai mampu mengatur waktu dan memenuhi tuntutan akademik dengan lebih baik

title: Estrés académico en estudiantes de práctica clínica del consultorio psicológico solidario de la Universidad Cooperativa de Colombia campus Villavicencio
abstract:
Este estudio investiga los niveles de estrés académico en estudiantes de psicología de la Universidad Cooperativa de Colombia, campus Villavicencio, un tema de creciente relevancia en la educación superior, particularmente en la formación clínica de psicólogos, el objetivo principal fue analizar los niveles de estrés académico y describir los síntomas asociados, empleando el Inventario SISCO SV-21 como herramienta para evaluar estresores, síntomas y estrategias de afrontamiento. Metodológicamente se adoptó un enfoque cuantitativo y un diseño no experimental descriptivo, la recolección de datos se realizó a través del Inventario SISCO-SV 21, aplicado a una muestra de 57 estudiantes de práctica clínica del consultorio psicológico. Los resultados evidenciaron que la mayoría de los estudiantes universitarios (57.89%) experimentan estrés académico severo, influenciado por factores sociodemográficos como edad, 
género, nivel socioeconómico y las condiciones de desplazamiento desde el lugar de origen, a lo largo del análisis, se confirmó que el estrés académico es el resultado de múltiples factores que convergen en el estudiante universitario: exigencias institucionales, falta de tiempo, presión por resultados, condiciones económicas precarias, responsabilidades familiares y laborales, y limitada 
preparación emocional para enfrentar situaciones clínicas reales.His study investigates the levels of academic stress in psychology students at the Cooperativa University of Colombia, Villavicencio campus, a topic of increasing relevance in higher education, particularly in the clinical training of psychologists. The main objective was to analyze the levels of academic stress and describe the associated symptoms, employing the SISCO SV-21 Inventory as a tool to evaluate stressors, symptoms, and coping strategies. Methodologically, a quantitative approach and a non-experimental descriptive design were adopted. Data collection was carried out through the SISCO-SV 21 Inventory, applied to a sample of 57 clinical practice students from the psychological counseling center. The results showed that the majority of university students (57.89%) experience severe academic stress, influenced by sociodemographic factors such as age, gender, socioeconomic level, and displacement conditions from their place of origin. Throughout the analysis, it was confirmed that academic stress is the result of multiple factors that converge in the university student: institutional demands, lack of time, pressure for results, precarious economic conditions, family and work responsibilities, and limited emotional preparation to face real clinical situations.1. title -English: Academic Stress Among Students in Clinical Practicum at the Solidarity Psychology Clinic of the Cooperative University of Colombia, Villavicencio Campus
abstract - English:
This study investigates levels of academic stress among psychology students at the Cooperative University of Colombia, Villavicencio campus—an issue of growing relevance in higher education, particularly in the clinical training of psychologists. The main objective was to analyze levels of academic stress and describe the associated symptoms, using the SISCO SV-21 Inventory as a tool to assess stressors, symptoms, and coping strategies. Methodologically, a quantitative approach and a descriptive, non-experimental design were adopted. Data were collected using the SISCO-SV-21 Inventory, administered to a sample of 57 students participating in clinical practice at the psychology clinic. The results showed that the majority of college students (57.89%) experience severe academic stress, influenced by sociodemographic factors such as age, 
gender, socioeconomic status, and the conditions of their commute from their place of origin. Throughout the analysis, it was confirmed that academic stress is the result of multiple factors that converge on college students: institutional demands, lack of time, pressure to achieve results, precarious economic conditions, family and work responsibilities, and limited 
emotional preparedness to deal with real clinical situations. title: Dampak perceraian orang tua terhadap prestasi akademik siswa Kelas VIII di MTs Al Maarif Desa Bocek Kecamatan Karangploso Kabupaten Malang
abstract:
INDONESIA:



Masa remaja merupakan masa transisi yang penuh tantangan dan gejolak emosi, sehingga kehadiran dan peran orang tua sangat krusial dalam membentuk karakter dan prestasi anak, termasuk dalam pendidikan formal. Ketika perceraian terjadi, stabilitas emosi anak dan perhatian dari kedua orang tua seringkali terganggu, yang pada akhirnya dapat memengaruhi proses belajar dan hasil akademiknya. Penelitian ini bertujuan untuk menganalisis secara mendalam dampak perceraian orang tua terhadap prestasi akademik siswa kelas VIII MTs Al Maarif, Desa Bocek, Kecamatan Karangploso, Kabupaten Malang. Perceraian merupakan fenomena sosial yang berkembang dengan konsekuensi psikologis dan sosial yang kompleks, terutama bagi remaja.



Penelitian ini menggunakan pendekatan kualitatif deskriptif, dengan pengumpulan data melalui observasi langsung, wawancara mendalam dengan siswa, guru bimbingan dan konseling (BK), wali kelas, dan dokumentasi. Subjek penelitian adalah siswa kelas delapan dari keluarga yang mengalami perceraian. Para peneliti berupaya mengeksplorasi pengalaman subjektif siswa dalam menghadapi perceraian orang tua dan mengkaji hubungannya dengan prestasi akademik, kesejahteraan mental dan emosional, serta dukungan sosial yang mereka terima.



Hasil penelitian menunjukkan bahwa perceraian orang tua berdampak negatif terhadap sebagian besar siswa, baik secara akademik maupun psikologis. Beberapa siswa menunjukkan penurunan nilai akademik yang signifikan, kesulitan berkonsentrasi di kelas, dan kehilangan minat belajar. Secara psikologis, siswa mengalami berbagai gangguan, seperti kesedihan yang berkepanjangan, rasa takut ditinggalkan, kecemasan, rasa malu di sekitar teman, dan rasa percaya diri yang rendah. Namun, dampak-dampak ini tidak seragam. Beberapa siswa mampu mengatasi tekanan-tekanan ini dengan menunjukkan ketahanan emosional yang kuat, terutama berkat dukungan dari sekolah, teman sebaya, atau keluarga besar mereka yang peduli dengan situasi mereka.



Kesimpulan dari penelitian ini adalah bahwa perceraian orang tua dapat berdampak signifikan terhadap prestasi akademik siswa, tetapi tingkat dampak ini sangat bergantung pada kepribadian anak, strategi koping yang mereka gunakan, dan tingkat dukungan dari lingkungan mereka. Peran sekolah, khususnya konselor bimbingan dan wali kelas, sangat penting dalam memberikan dukungan psikologis dan akademik kepada siswa yang mengalami krisis keluarga. Dengan perhatian dan pendekatan yang tepat, siswa dari keluarga yang bercerai tetap memiliki kesempatan yang sama untuk meraih keberhasilan akademik dan berkembang secara optimal di lingkungan pendidikan.



ENGLISH:



Adolescence is a transitional period full of challenges and emotional upheaval, so the presence and role of parents are crucial in shaping children's character and achievements, including in formal education. When divorce occurs, children's emotional stability and attention from both parents are often disrupted, which can ultimately affect their learning process and academic outcomes. This study aims to analyze in depth the impact of parental divorce on the academic achievement of eighth grade students at MTs Al Maarif, Bocek Village, Karangploso District, Malang Regency. Divorce is a growing social phenomenon with complex psychological and social consequences, especially for adolescents.



This study used a descriptive qualitative approach, collecting data through direct observation, in-depth interviews with students, guidance and counseling (BK) teachers, homeroom teachers, and documentation. The subjects were eighth-grade students from families experiencing divorce. The researchers sought to explore the students' subjective experiences of coping with parental divorce and examine its relationship to academic achievement, mental and emotional well-being, and the social support they received.



The results showed that parental divorce negatively impacted the majority of students, both academically and psychologically. Some students exhibited a significant decline in academic grades, difficulty concentrating in class, and a loss of interest in learning. Psychologically, students experienced various disturbances, such as prolonged sadness, fear of abandonment, anxiety, embarrassment around friends, and low self-confidence. However, these impacts were not uniform. Some students were able to overcome these pressures by demonstrating strong emotional resilience, primarily due to the support of their school, peers, or extended family who cared about their situation.



The conclusion of this study is that parental divorce can have a significant impact on students' academic achievement, but the extent of this impact depends heavily on the child's personality, the coping strategies they use, and the level of support from their environment. The role of schools, particularly guidance counselors and homeroom teachers, is crucial in providing psychological and academic support to students experiencing family crises. With the right attention and approach, students from divorced families still have equal opportunities to achieve academic success and develop optimally in the educational environment.



ARABIC:



تُعدّ مرحلة المراهقة فترة انتقالية مليئة بالتحديات والاضطرابات العاطفية، لذا فإن وجود الوالدين ودورهما حاسمان في تشكيل شخصية الطفل وإنجازاته، بما في ذلك في التعليم الرسمي. عند حدوث الطلاق، غالبًا ما يضطرب الاستقرار العاطفي للأطفال واهتمام كلا الوالدين بهم، مما قد يؤثر في النهاية على عملية تعلمهم ونتائجهم الأكاديمية. تهدف هذه الدراسة إلى تحليل متعمق لأثر طلاق الوالدين على التحصيل الدراسي لطلاب الصف الثامن في مدرسة المعارف المتوسطة، قرية بوتشيك، مقاطعة كارانغبلوسو، مقاطعة مالانغ. يُعد الطلاق ظاهرة اجتماعية متنامية تحمل عواقب نفسية واجتماعية معقدة، لا سيما على المراهقين.



اعتمدت هذه الدراسة على منهج وصفي نوعي، وجمعت البيانات من خلال الملاحظة المباشرة، والمقابلات المعمقة مع الطلاب، ومعلمي التوجيه والإرشاد، ومعلمي الصفوف، والتوثيق. شملت الدراسة طلاب الصف الثامن من أسر تعاني من الطلاق. سعى الباحثون إلى استكشاف التجارب الذاتية للطلاب في التعامل مع طلاق الوالدين، ودراسة علاقته بالتحصيل الدراسي، والصحة النفسية والعاطفية، والدعم الاجتماعي الذي تلقوه.



أظهرت النتائج أن طلاق الوالدين أثر سلبًا على غالبية الطلاب، أكاديميًا ونفسيًا. أظهر بعض الطلاب انخفاضًا ملحوظًا في الدرجات الأكاديمية، وصعوبة في التركيز في الفصل، وفقدانًا للاهتمام بالتعلم. من الناحية النفسية، عانى الطلاب من اضطرابات مختلفة، كالحزن المطول، والخوف من الهجر، والقلق، والشعور بالحرج من الأصدقاء، وانخفاض الثقة بالنفس. ومع ذلك، لم تكن هذه الآثار موحدة. فقد تمكن بعض الطلاب من التغلب على هذه الضغوط بإظهار مرونة عاطفية قوية، ويعود ذلك أساسًا إلى دعم المدرسة أو الأقران أو العائلة الممتدة التي اهتمت بحالتهم.



وخلصت هذه الدراسة إلى أن طلاق الوالدين يمكن أن يكون له تأثير كبير على التحصيل الدراسي للطلاب، إلا أن مدى هذا التأثير يعتمد بشكل كبير على شخصية الطفل، واستراتيجيات التكيف التي يستخدمها، ومستوى الدعم من بيئته. ويُعد دور المدارس، وخاصةً مرشدي التوجيه ومعلمي الصفوف، بالغ الأهمية في تقديم الدعم النفسي والأكاديمي للطلاب الذين يمرون بأزمات أسرية. ومع الاهتمام والنهج المناسبين، لا يزال الطلاب من الأسر المطلقة يتمتعون بفرص متساوية لتحقيق النجاح الأكاديمي والتطور الأمثل في البيئة التعليمي
title -English: The Impact of Parental Divorce on the Academic Achievement of Eighth-Grade Students at MTs Al Maarif in Bocek Village, Karangploso Subdistrict, Malang Regency
abstract - English:
INDONESIA Adolescence is a transitional period full of challenges and emotional turmoil, making the presence and role of parents crucial in shaping a child’s character and achievements, including in formal education. When divorce occurs, a child’s emotional stability and the attention they receive from both parents are often disrupted, which can ultimately affect their learning process and academic performance. This study aims to conduct an in-depth analysis of the impact of parental divorce on the academic achievement of eighth-grade students at MTs Al Maarif, Bocek Village, Karangploso Subdistrict, Malang Regency. Divorce is a growing social phenomenon with complex psychological and social consequences, particularly for adolescents.



This study employs a descriptive qualitative approach, with data collected through direct observation, in-depth interviews with students, guidance and counseling (BK) teachers, homeroom teachers, and documentation. The research subjects were eighth-grade students from families experiencing divorce. The researchers sought to explore the students’ subjective experiences in coping with their parents’ divorce and to examine the relationship between these experiences and their academic performance, mental and emotional well-being, and the social support they received.



Research findings indicate that parental divorce has a negative impact on most students, both academically and psychologically. Some students show a significant decline in academic performance, difficulty concentrating in class, and a loss of interest in learning. Psychologically, students experience various disturbances, such as prolonged sadness, fear of abandonment, anxiety, embarrassment around friends, and low self-esteem. However, these effects are not uniform. Some students are able to cope with these pressures by demonstrating strong emotional resilience, particularly thanks to support from their school, peers, or extended family members who care about their situation.



The conclusion of this study is that parental divorce can have a significant impact on students’ academic performance, but the extent of this impact depends heavily on the child’s personality, the coping strategies they employ, and the level of support from their environment. The role of the school—particularly guidance counselors and homeroom teachers—is crucial in providing psychological and academic support to students experiencing family crises. With the right attention and approach, students from divorced families still have an equal opportunity to achieve academic success and thrive in an educational setting.                                                                                                             ENGLISH:



Adolescence is a transitional period full of challenges and emotional upheaval, so the presence and role of parents are crucial in shaping children's character and achievements, including in formal education. When divorce occurs, children's emotional stability and attention from both parents are often disrupted, which can ultimately affect their learning process and academic outcomes. This study aims to analyze in depth the impact of parental divorce on the academic achievement of eighth grade students at MTs Al Maarif, Bocek Village, Karangploso District, Malang Regency. Divorce is a growing social phenomenon with complex psychological and social consequences, especially for adolescents.



This study used a descriptive qualitative approach, collecting data through direct observation, in-depth interviews with students, guidance and counseling (BK) teachers, homeroom teachers, and documentation. The subjects were eighth-grade students from families experiencing divorce. The researchers sought to explore the students' subjective experiences of coping with parental divorce and examine its relationship to academic achievement, mental and emotional well-being, and the social support they received.



The results showed that parental divorce negatively impacted the majority of students, both academically and psychologically. Some students exhibited a significant decline in academic grades, difficulty concentrating in class, and a loss of interest in learning. Psychologically, students experienced various disturbances, such as prolonged sadness, fear of abandonment, anxiety, embarrassment around friends, and low self-confidence. However, these impacts were not uniform. Some students were able to overcome these pressures by demonstrating strong emotional resilience, primarily due to the support of their school, peers, or extended family who cared about their situation.



The conclusion of this study is that parental divorce can have a significant impact on students' academic achievement, but the extent of this impact depends heavily on the child's personality, the coping strategies they use, and the level of support from their environment. The role of schools, particularly guidance counselors and homeroom teachers, is crucial in providing psychological and academic support to students experiencing family crises. With the right attention and approach, students from divorced families still have equal opportunities to achieve academic success and develop optimally in the educational environment.
